entries.definition:
gh–ḍ–ḍ to be tender, to be young; to be affluent; to cast
one’s eyes down, to show humility, to be modest, to check. Of
this root, only يَغُضُّ yaghuḍḍ occurs four times in the Qur’an.

يَغُضُّ yaghuḍḍ [imperf. v. trans.] 1 to lower (one’s voice)
(31:19) وَاقْصِدْ فِي مَشْيِكَ وَاغْضُضْ مِنْ صَوْتِكَ إِنَّ أَنْكَرَ الْأَصْوَاتِ لَصَوْتُ الْحَمِيرِ
go at a moderate pace and lower your voice, for the ugliest of all
voices is the braying of asses 2 to cast down (the gaze) (24:31) وَقُلْ
لِلْمُؤْمِنَاتِ يَغْضُضْنَ مِنْ أَبْصَارِهِنَّ and tell believing women that they
should lower their gaze [be modest].
